claim: Mg-0.4Zn1Y alloy exhibits higher strength when prepared via rapid solidification compared to casting.
ZnxMg1-xO alloys are p-type dopable within the stable rocksalt structure, while also exhibiting a band gap greater than 4eV.
New lightweight scandium alloy exhibits improved oxidation resistance from chromium doping.
New lightweight scandium alloy exhibits improved mechanical properties from iron doping.
New lightweight scandium alloy exhibits improved oxidation resistance from aluminum doping.
New lightweight scandium alloy exhibits improved oxidation resistance from yttrium doping.
Gallium dopant enhances fracture toughness of iron alloy through controlled precipitation hardening.
Gallium dopant enhances fracture toughness of iron alloy at cryogenic temperatures.
Aluminum dopant enhances fracture toughness of iron alloy.
A novel lightweight alloy is strengthened by precipitates of a metastable phase with composition close to Ti2Al3 that forms under extreme non-equilibrium processing conditions.

A novel lightweight alloy is strengthened by precipitates of a novel metastable phase with a tetragonal structure similar to known Ti-Al compounds that has been predicted theoretically using density functional theory.

A novel lightweight alloy is strengthened by precipitates of a novel metastable phase with composition near TiAl2 and tetragonal structure that has been predicted theoretically using density functional theory.

A novel lightweight alloy is strengthened by precipitates of a novel metastable phase based on the known TiAl3 structure with minor compositional modifications that has been predicted theoretically using density functional theory.
Superconductivity in A15-type V-Nb-Mo-Sn-Ge high entropy alloys have been observed with a maximum Tc of 8K.
Novel A15-type La-H system exhibits high-temperature superconductivity below 50GPa.
A novel variation of the Al-Nb-Ti-V-Zr multicomponent alloy has been used to make superconducting wires with a Tc of 9.43K.
